hero.tagline: Multilingual ski & snowboard lessons in Niseko
hero.subtitle: JD Snowdrift & Summer Sports offers a wide range of lessons and tours across Niseko, Rusutsu, and Kiroro
hero.intro: Want to ski in Hokkaido but don't know where to start? JD designs ski lessons for Mandarin, Cantonese, and other Chinese-speaking guests from Taiwan, Hong Kong, Malaysia and beyond. Our team are internationally certified instructors who train more than 8 hours a week, so every student learns safely and happily under expert guidance and falls in love with skiing. JD also offers one-stop travel support — hotels, transfers, and gear rental can all be arranged for you. Come find JD in Hokkaido and experience world-class powder!

lift.heading: Lift Status
about.heading: About JD
about.body: JD Snowdrift & Summer Sports offers a wide range of lessons and tours across Niseko, Rusutsu, and Kiroro: private ski lessons (absolute beginners, kids' lessons, family lessons, advanced skill-building, instructor exam prep), CASI Hokkaido snowboard instructor exams (co-organizer), and summer outdoor activities (SUP paddleboarding, custom cycling tours, mountain biking).
guide.mountain.heading: Hokkaido Resort Guide
guide.mountain.niseko: Niseko United is the collective name for four resorts — Grand Hirafu, Annupuri, Hanazono, and Niseko Village — a world-famous powder paradise with varied terrain and great amenities, suitable from beginner to expert. A must-visit destination for any serious skier.
guide.mountain.rusutsu: Rusutsu Resort is a fully-equipped, family-friendly ski-in/ski-out resort with a theme-park-like atmosphere and a wide range of snow activities — a top choice for family skiing.
guide.mountain.kiroro: Kiroro Resort is a relatively quiet resort with excellent snow quality and fewer crowds, ideal for skiers who prefer a relaxed pace.
guide.gear.heading: Ski Clothing & Gear Guide
guide.gear.body: Outer layer: a proper ski jacket and pants with basic waterproofing, breathability, and warmth. Mid layer: moisture-wicking base layers — skiing is intense, so staying dry matters. Inner layer: add a down jacket if needed in extreme cold. Rental suggestions: skis/board, bindings, poles, boots, ski pants/jacket, helmet (we offer basic goggle rental for lesson use only, ¥1,000/day, limited stock). Purchase suggestions: boots, waterproof gloves, goggles, protective gear. Beginners should rent first and consider buying once they're more familiar with the sport.
guide.family.heading: Family Skiing Guide
guide.family.body: We recommend a minimum age of 4 — bone and muscle development is mature enough by then for safer, faster progress. For complete beginners, we recommend teaching adults and children separately, since kids tend to progress faster than adults, which can otherwise lead to a frustrating experience for the adult. For beginner-to-intermediate skiers, our senior instructors can guide the whole family together for a safe, fun adventure on the mountain.
